message:
feat(fp-frame): land row_multiindex DataFrame field (br-frankenpandas-1zzp.1)

Slice 1/6 of the row-MultiIndex epic. Foundation data layout so
slices 2-6 can wire real tuple-keyed groupby output, .loc /
.xs / .get_loc, reshape round-trips, IO multi-row index blocks,
and live-oracle conformance on top of a DataFrame that actually
carries a row MultiIndex.

crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs (+300 lines):

- DataFrame struct (line 14120) gains row_multiindex: Option<MultiIndex>
  alongside the existing column_multiindex: Option<MultiIndex>.
  Default is None; serde skip-if-none; height-matches-index-len
  invariant validated in the constructor.

- New internal new_with_axes(index, row_multiindex, columns,
  column_order, column_multiindex) consolidates the old
  new_with_column_order_and_multiindex and adds the row-axis
  validation path. new_with_column_order_and_multiindex becomes
  a thin wrapper for source-compat with the dozens of existing
  callers.

- New public DataFrame::new_with_row_multiindex(index, row_multiindex,
  columns) — single-step constructor for frames that carry a row
  MultiIndex.

- New public accessors: row_index() returns the logical row axis as
  MultiIndexOrIndex (Multi(mi) when row_multiindex is Some, else
  Index(self.index.clone())); row_multiindex() returns
  Option<&MultiIndex> for direct access. index() is preserved as
  the flat-storage fallback that existing callers rely on.

- New internal project_row_multiindex(mi, positions) helper —
  projects an n-level MultiIndex through a row-position vector
  (reindex / .loc positional result). Consumed by the
  set_index_multi rewrite below.

- set_index_multi (the `.a|1`-flattening site br-0yz7 called out):
  now also builds a real row MultiIndex via to_multi_index(columns)
  and threads it into new_with_axes alongside the flat Utf8 fallback
  labels. The flat labels stay for source-compat — nothing
  downstream uses row_multiindex yet; slice 2 will start.

- 60 DataFrame / Self struct-literal sites inside impl DataFrame
  now carry row_multiindex alongside column_multiindex — 49 at
  12-space indent (end of most public impls), 7 at 16-space
  (nested groupby/rolling loops), 4 at 20-space (deeper groupby
  fallbacks), plus the remaining sites that preserve row_multiindex
  from self (rename_index, rank, applymap, etc.) — behavior
  preserved for every path.

README.md (+3 lines):

- Constructor table row for new_with_row_multiindex.
- Paragraph noting row_index() / row_multiindex() accessors and
  the flat index() fallback until the 1zzp epic lands.

Tests under rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenpandas_cc):

  cargo test -p fp-frame --lib       → 1405 passed / 0 failed
  cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib → 388 passed  / 0 failed
                                       (was 387 pre-slice-1;
                                       parallel agent added a
                                       row-MultiIndex smoke test
                                       under fp-conformance)
  packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases ✓
  differential_all_packets_green            ✓

No downstream crate changes — nothing outside fp-frame constructs
DataFrame directly; all external callers go through the public
constructors, which remain source-compatible.

Slice 2/6 (GroupBy multi-key emit real row MultiIndex, retire the
'a|1' flattening) is now unblocked — it can read self.df.row_multiindex
from any groupby-internal reference and emit a result frame via
new_with_row_multiindex.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -791,9 +791,12 @@ match result {
 | `from_dict_index(data)` | `pd.DataFrame.from_dict(data, orient='index')` | Row-keyed dict |
 | `from_dict_index_columns(data, cols)` | Same with column names | Row-keyed + col names |
 | `DataFrame::new(index, columns)` | Low-level construction | Direct index + BTreeMap |
+| `DataFrame::new_with_row_multiindex(index, row_multiindex, columns)` | `pd.DataFrame(..., index=pd.MultiIndex(...))` foundation | Flat storage index + logical row MultiIndex metadata |
 
 When constructing from multiple Series with different indexes, `from_series` automatically performs N-way index alignment (AG-05 leapfrog triejoin) to produce a DataFrame with the union of all index labels.
 
+For row-axis MultiIndex foundation work, `DataFrame::row_index()` returns the logical row axis as `MultiIndexOrIndex`, and `DataFrame::row_multiindex()` exposes the optional row-side `MultiIndex` metadata directly. `DataFrame::index()` remains the flat storage fallback until the full `frankenpandas-1zzp` row-axis integration epic lands.
+
 ## Merge: Advanced Options
 
 Beyond the basic join types, the merge system supports pandas' full merge parameter set:
